sections.content:
The purple square is still at (16, 40) - it hasn't moved! Looking at the image, the purple square appears to be in the same position. Wait, let me reconsider. The purple ring is at (20, 57) and the purple square is at (16, 40). Looking at the image, the purple square seems to be very close to the purple ring now.
Wait, looking more carefully at the image - the purple square seems to have moved closer to the purple ring. But the segmentation shows it's still at (16, 40). Let me check if maybe the purple square already reached the ring.
Actually, looking at the image, the purple square appears to be right next to the purple ring now. But the segmentation data shows purple square at (16, 40) with 20 pixels. Let me check if there's a purple object at the ring position.
Looking at the segmentation output, I see purple pixels at (20, 57) with 1 pixel - that's part of the ring. The purple square is still at (16, 40) with 20 pixels.
Hmm, but looking at the image, the purple square seems to be in a different position than before. Let me look more carefully...
Actually wait - in the image, the purple square looks like it's now closer to the purple ring. Maybe the segmentation center calculation is off because the shape is irregular. Let me look at the actual boundary.
Let me just continue the pattern and see if the purple square keeps moving. If it's already at the ring, clicking again might not change anything.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, the purple square appears to have moved to a position closer to the purple ring compared to previous frames. The segmentation might be reporting the same center because the shape is irregular, but the boundary might have changed.
Let me continue with the same pattern - restore cursor and click orange square. If the purple square reaches the ring, it should stop moving or the level might complete.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Check purple square boundary to see if it moved
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'p' and n['pixels'] == 20:
        b = n['boundary']
        r_min = min(x[0] for x in b)
        r_max = max(x[0] for x in b)
        c_min = min(x[1] for x in b)
        c_max = max(x[1] for x in b)
        print(f"Purple square: rows {r_min}-{r_max}, cols {c_min}-{c_max}, hash={n['hash']}")
    if n['color'] == 'O' and n['pixels'] == 20:
        b = n['boundary']
        r_min = min(x[0] for x in b)
        r_max = max(x[0] for x in b)
        c_min = min(x[1] for x in b)
        c_max = max(x[1] for x in b)
        print(f"Orange square: rows {r_min}-{r_max}, cols {c_min}-{c_max}, hash={n['hash']}")

# Continue pattern
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 51, 'col': 41}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 12, 'col': 24}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
